title: Lever 3: Event-driven trade filtering (FOMC, CPI, NFP)
why: Gold has structurally predictable directional behavior around macro repricing events. Filtering trades to ±1 day from FOMC/CPI/NFP reduces events from ~250/y to 30-40/y, but with much higher signal-to-noise. Mechanism: macro repricing causes large moves; the rest is noise + market-making. Currently the pipeline has FRED macro features but no explicit calendar-based event filter.
value_rationale: M (clean mechanism; lower event count than B0015/B0016)
scope: new `pipeline/event_calendar.py` ingesting Fed meeting dates (FOMC minutes publication), BLS NFP/CPI release dates. Add feature `bars_to_next_macro_event` (negative if in post-event window, positive if pre-event). Build proposal "Long XAU on next-day after FOMC release when (some condition)". DA-review under the new calendar category (B0020).
